cross_scene_arcs.title: Mentorship and Influence Over Younger Players
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Annie guides younger players, especially Nuke, through emotional, physical, and metaphysical methods, reinforcing her role as a mentor while navigating the limits of influence and intimacy.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Annie asserts authority among the groupies and corrects Millie's passivity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Annie accepts the end of her relationship with Nuke as he moves to the majors, reflecting on whether he internalized her teachings.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether her mentorship had lasting impact beyond ritual and superstition

cross_scene_arcs.title: Romantic Tension and Emotional Conflict with Crash Davis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Annie's dynamic with Crash evolves from flirtation to emotional entanglement, marked by unmet expectations, sudden departures, and eventual mutual decision to embrace presence over pursuit.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Annie becomes the focal point of competition between Crash and Ebby.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Annie chooses to stay with Crash in a quiet, undefined moment, signaling emotional maturity and shared stillness.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: future of their non-traditional relationship and depth of mutual commitment

cross_scene_arcs.title: Struggle Between Personal Agency and Romantic Involvement
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Annie repeatedly confronts the tension between maintaining personal agency and becoming emotionally entangled in transient romantic relationships with players.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Annie emphasizes personal responsibility in Millie while asserting control in the group.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Annie questions whether baseball and fleeting relationships are still enough, signaling a desire for deeper connection.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether she can sustain fulfillment outside of romantic and sexual dynamics with players

cross_scene_arcs.title: Transition from Player Relationships to Personal Fulfillment
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Annie moves from cyclical affairs with young players toward a more introspective, self-defined life, choosing emotional presence over romantic pursuit.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Annie expresses doubt about continuing relationships with younger players and reflects on her identity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Annie commits to quitting relationships with 'boys' and aligns with Crash’s decision to step away from the game.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how she will redefine her purpose beyond baseball and romance

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mentorship and Control Over Nuke LaLoosh
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Crash transitions from reluctant mentor to dominant authority figure over Nuke, enforcing discipline and shaping his development, but ultimately resents being replaced as Nuke rises.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: reluctantly assigned to mentor a raw, undisciplined rookie
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: emotionally destabilized after Nuke's promotion, lashing out in drunken confrontation
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Crash's resentment over being surpassed by a younger player he helped mold

cross_scene_arcs.title: Romantic Pursuit and Emotional Intimacy with Annie
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Crash moves from making a bold romantic advance to sharing an intimate morning after and ultimately choosing emotional presence with Annie over baseball ambition.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: initiates romantic tension with a direct, unfiltered note
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: retires from baseball and embraces a quiet, intimate life with Annie
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether he can sustain fulfillment outside of baseball and in a committed relationship

cross_scene_arcs.title: Professional Identity and Career Transition
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Crash resists demotion and asserts veteran status through defiance and discipline, but ultimately faces release and chooses retirement, transitioning from player to retired figure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: demoted to A-ball and assigned a developmental role against his will
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: officially released and retires after hitting his final home run
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: his future role in baseball and personal identity beyond the game remain uncertain

cross_scene_arcs.title: Defiance and Rule-Bending as Veteran Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Crash repeatedly asserts independence through physical dominance, rule-breaking, and public confrontation, establishing himself as a defiant veteran who operates on his own terms.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: uses physical force to establish authority over Nuke
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: ejected from a game after escalating a verbal confrontation with an umpire
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: his emotional volatility and defiance continue to jeopardize his standing and influence

cross_scene_arcs.title: Struggle for Professional Legitimacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ebby prioritizes image and social performance over professional discipline, undermining his credibility as a player while seeking validation through charisma and attention.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: unrepentant and unprepared rookie facing managerial reprimand
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: gains social visibility but reinforces perception as a disruptive presence
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: lack of accountability and discipline threatens career readiness

cross_scene_arcs.title: Pursuit of Validation Through Image
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ebby consistently seeks approval not through performance or professionalism, but through nickname adoption, physical display, and social spectacle.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: seeking validation through image (nickname) rather than performance
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: gains Annie's admiration through dance, reinforcing preference for image over substance
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: lack of focus on skill development and team integration
